creator: lysfjord.daniel@smokepit.net
text:
(In reply to Cy Schubert from comment #4)

Architecture   : FreeBSD:12:amd64
Prefix         : /usr/local
Categories     : sysutils
Licenses       : LGPL21+, GPLv2+
Maintainer     : cy@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : http://www.syslog-ng.org/
Comment        : Powerful syslogd replacement

	AMQP           : off

Annotations    :
	FreeBSD_version: 1202000
	cpe            : cpe:2.3:a:oneidentity:syslog-ng:3.35.1:::::freebsd12:x64
	repo_type      : binary
	repository     : poudriere
Flat size      : 4.09MiB
Description    :
syslog-ng is an enhanced log daemon, supporting a wide range of input and
output methods: syslog, unstructured text, message queues, databases (SQL
and NoSQL alike) and more.

Key features:

  * receive and send RFC3164 and RFC5424 style syslog messages
  * work with any kind of unstructured data
  * receive and send JSON formatted messages
  * classify and structure logs with builtin parsers (csv-parser(),
    db-parser(), ...)
  * normalize, crunch and process logs as they flow through the system
  * hand on messages for further processing using message queues (like
    AMQP), files or databases (like PostgreSQL or MongoDB).

The official home page of syslog-ng is:
WWW: http://www.syslog-ng.org/
